# Markdown 67

> A free, client-side Markdown editor with synced visual (Notion-style) and raw panels. Everything runs in your browser — no account, no server, no upload.

Markdown 67 lets you write Markdown without memorizing syntax: type in a visual editor or in raw Markdown, kept perfectly in sync, then export a ready-to-use `.md` file. Your document never leaves your device — drafts are saved locally in the browser for 72 hours. The editor is desktop-focused (needs a viewport of at least 1024px).

## Pages
- [Home / editor](https://markdown67.app/): The editor app.
- [About](https://markdown67.app/about): What it is, how it works, features, and FAQ.

## Key facts
- Free, no account, no sign-up, no email.
- 100% client-side: nothing is uploaded to a server; drafts stored in localStorage for 72 hours.
- Two synced editors: a visual (WYSIWYG, BlockNote) panel and a raw Markdown panel — the Markdown is always the source of truth.
- Export/download `.md`, copy to clipboard, upload and drag-drop `.md` files.
- Supports GitHub Flavored Markdown (headings, lists, tables, code blocks, quotes, links, emphasis).
- Built by Romano Battiti. Hosted on Vercel.
